deliverable: Module 4 Part A — Presentation: Tommy (ACEs) — 'Relational Disruption to Relational Repair'
note: These entries resolve curriculum-internal pointers to proper academic sources for ideas being considered for the deck. They are NOT yet cited on any slide (status: resolved-pending-use). Do NOT cite the curriculum pointer (e.g. 'M4 W5 P64') on the reference slide — cite the resolved academic source.

entries.concept: Over-medicalisation / 'therapeutic ethos' critique — caution against pathologising ordinary distress and multiplying disorder labels; risk that a therapeutic focus diminishes resilience/autonomy.
entries.intendedUse: Slide 3 (school-age behaviours: aggression + inattention) OR the complexity/diagnostic-overshadowing slide — one evaluative sentence cautioning against mislabelling Tommy's adaptations to toxic stress as ADHD/ODD/conduct disorder.
entries.source: Ecclestone, K. and Hayes, D. (2019) The Dangerous Rise of Therapeutic Education. 2nd edn. London: Routledge. (1st edn 2009). doi:10.4324/9780429401039
entries.secondarySupport: Montessori's reframing that 'many issues traditionally seen as medical were educational' (P64).

entries.concept: 'Reparation' — treating at-risk children not as the problem but as individuals who faced hardship; repair the environment (stability, belonging, routine). Historical ancestor of trauma-informed / attachment-aware practice and the deck's 'Relational Repair' spine.
entries.intendedUse: Slide 4-5 (Corrective Experiences / Path Forward) — already the backbone of the deck; Aichhorn gives it a named historical anchor.
entries.source: Aichhorn, A. (1935) Wayward Youth. New York: Viking Press. (orig. Verwahrloste Jugend, 1925).
entries.secondarySupport: Curriculum secondary source: Winship, G. (history of mental health in schools).

entries.concept: Formulation over diagnosis — '5Ps' model; the predisposing-factor question is literally 'What has happened to the young person in the past?' (i.e. 'what happened to you', not 'what's wrong with you').
entries.intendedUse: Supports the over-medicalisation counter-move on Slide 3 and the repair framing on Slide 4.
entries.source: Macneil, C.A. et al. (2012) 'Is diagnosis enough to guide interventions in mental health? Using case formulation in clinical practice', BMC Medicine, 10, 111. doi:10.1186/1741-7015-10-111; plus Anna Freud Centre (case formulation) and Kempen (formulation-as-roadmap).

entries.concept: Trauma & neurobiological development — latent vulnerability (threat/reward/memory systems); recovery is possible with the right support. Directly underpins Tommy's amygdala-hyperactivation / PFC-suppression mechanism and the 'not deterministic' message.
entries.intendedUse: Slides 2-3 (biological bedrock / behaviours) and the 'probabilistic not deterministic' caveat on the ACE slide.
entries.source: McCrory, E.J. and Viding, E. (2015) 'The theory of latent vulnerability: Reconceptualizing the link between childhood maltreatment and psychiatric disorder', Development and Psychopathology, 27(2), pp. 493-505. doi:10.1017/S0954579415000115; plus Anna Freud Centre and Cross et al.
